    Purchase options and add-ons

    Sign in to continue
    • LEROBOT & ALOHA COMPATIBLE FOR EMBODIED AI — Synria robotic arm fully supports LeRobot framework and Aloha reproduction for ROS1/ROS2 embodied intelligence deep learning research. Compatible with Hugging Face, enables imitation learning and AI visual recognition experiments. Perfect for robotics labs, university research, and AI developers advancing embodied intelligence applications.
    • 6DOF WITH 650MM REACH & 1000G PAYLOAD — Features six degrees of freedom with 650mm arm reach and impressive payload capacity up to 1000g (high-load version). Equipped with intelligent serial bus servos (UART protocol), delivers 1.0mm repeatability precision and 12+ hours continuous operation. Premium aluminum extrusion construction with industrial-grade bearings ensures long-term reliability.
    • DUAL-ARM TELEOPERATION SYSTEM — Includes execution arm and controller arm for intuitive teleoperation with XT30 sync cable. Supports multiple control methods: PC software, mobile APP, wireless controller, and direct Python programming. Beginner-friendly interface with comprehensive tutorials enables students and engineers to start quickly, even without prior robotics experience.
    • FULL ROS1/ROS2 & MOVEIT INTEGRATION — Complete ROS1 and ROS2 support with accurate URDF models, driver nodes, and motion planning via MoveIt framework. STM32 microprocessor enables high-performance serial bus servo control. Supports Gazebo simulation for algorithm testing and AI training without physical hardware. Compatible with Python, C++, and open-source development environments.
    • MULTI-KIT OPTIONS FOR STEM EDUCATION & RESEARCH — Available in Single-arm, double-arm, four-arm configurations to meet different project needs. Designed for STEM education, university courses, and advanced AI research. Dedicated technical support from product engineers with open-source code and tutorials on Sparkling Skills Sharing Platform. Ideal for robotics labs, engineering projects, and AI algorithm development.

      2. About Synria Robotics

        Synria Robotics Co., Ltd. (hereafter "Synria Robotics Tech") is a pioneering technology firm specializing in integrated embodied intelligence solutions. We are dedicated to the research, development, and delivery of high-precision, high-reliability teleoperated robotic arms and cutting-edge AI-driven remote interaction humanoid robots, providing exceptional technology and service to our industry clients.

      4. 6DOF Intelligent Robotic Arm

        The relevant materials are available on our GitHub repository. And you can download and modify it as needed.

        Where can I find information about company's products?

        Visit our company website to learn more about our products through our brand story.

        Does the product support secondary development?

        Our product is entirely open-source.

    • 1x Controller Arm；1x Execution Arm

      • Important information

      Safety Information

      No Warning Applicable

      Directions

      1. Remove the robotic arm kit from its packaging. 2. Follow the assembly guide provided on the SparkMind online learning platform to assemble the robotic arm. 3. Connect the robotic arm to a compatible device (e.g., Nvidia Jetson, LeRobot platform) using the appropriate cables. 4. Install the required software and drivers. 5. Power on the robotic arm and follow the initial setup instructions. 6. Begin using the robotic arm for AI education, research, or practical applications as described in the product details.

      How customer reviews and ratings work

      Customer Reviews, including Product Star Ratings help customers to learn more about the product and decide whether it is the right product for them.

      To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyzed reviews to verify trustworthiness.

      Product Summary: Synria AI Robotic Arm Kit 6DOF 650mm Reach 1kg Payload, LeRobot Aloha Compatible, ROS1/ROS2 MoveIt Support, AI Vision Learning, STEM Education with Synria C10 Camera

      From Synria Robotics

      50+ viewed in past month

      Price

      One-time purchase: $2,439.00

      Purchasing options

      Other sellers

      Other sellers

      About this Item

      • LEROBOT & ALOHA COMPATIBLE FOR EMBODIED AI — Synria robotic arm fully supports LeRobot framework and Aloha reproduction for ROS1/ROS2 embodied intelligence deep learning research. Compatible with Hugging Face, enables imitation learning and AI visual recognition experiments. Perfect for robotics labs, university research, and AI developers advancing embodied intelligence applications.
      • 6DOF WITH 650MM REACH & 1000G PAYLOAD — Features six degrees of freedom with 650mm arm reach and impressive payload capacity up to 1000g (high-load version). Equipped with intelligent serial bus servos (UART protocol), delivers 1.0mm repeatability precision and 12+ hours continuous operation. Premium aluminum extrusion construction with industrial-grade bearings ensures long-term reliability.
      • DUAL-ARM TELEOPERATION SYSTEM — Includes execution arm and controller arm for intuitive teleoperation with XT30 sync cable. Supports multiple control methods: PC software, mobile APP, wireless controller, and direct Python programming. Beginner-friendly interface with comprehensive tutorials enables students and engineers to start quickly, even without prior robotics experience.
      • FULL ROS1/ROS2 & MOVEIT INTEGRATION — Complete ROS1 and ROS2 support with accurate URDF models, driver nodes, and motion planning via MoveIt framework. STM32 microprocessor enables high-performance serial bus servo control. Supports Gazebo simulation for algorithm testing and AI training without physical hardware. Compatible with Python, C++, and open-source development environments.
      • MULTI-KIT OPTIONS FOR STEM EDUCATION & RESEARCH — Available in Single-arm, double-arm, four-arm configurations to meet different project needs. Designed for STEM education, university courses, and advanced AI research. Dedicated technical support from product engineers with open-source code and tutorials on Sparkling Skills Sharing Platform. Ideal for robotics labs, engineering projects, and AI algorithm development.

      Product Description

      The Lingdong Series is an embodied intelligent robotic arm designed for AI education scenarios, consisting of a teaching arm and an operational arm. Through advanced teleoperation and collaboration algorithms, it precisely maps the movements of the teaching controller to the operational arm, making it an ideal entry-level platform for robotic arm control, imitation learning, and end-to-end large model (VLA) research. The manipulator features a standard 6-DOF design with a total length of 796 mm and a reach of up to 650 mm, and can be optionally equipped with a high-definition camera. It is equipped with proprietary serial bus servos and aerospace-grade components to ensure stable and reliable performance. The first, second, and third joints are all driven by dual servos, with a standard payload capacity of 500 g and a heavy-duty version capable of handling up to 1000 g. The teaching arm features a similar design and is equipped with a dedicated trigger, supporting precise motion capture and remote operation. The system integrates the professional XuanYa SparkMind embodied intelligence algorithm platform, achieving seamless integration of vision, control, and mechanics. Users can easily implement functions such as coordinate control, motion planning, and collision detection. Combined with the Sparkling online learning platform, we provide systematic course content covering robotic arm simulation, forward and inverse kinematics, vision algorithms, and imitation learning. As a professional AI education platform, the Lingdong series prioritizes usability and practicality in both hardware and software design, and is fully open-source, supporting secondary development. Equipped with detailed Chinese tutorials and technical support, it provides an ideal practical platform for learners of AI and embodied intelligence.

      Important Information

      Safety Information

      No Warning Applicable

      Directions

      1. Remove the robotic arm kit from its packaging. 2. Follow the assembly guide provided on the SparkMind online learning platform to assemble the robotic arm. 3. Connect the robotic arm to a compatible device (e.g., Nvidia Jetson, LeRobot platform) using the appropriate cables. 4. Install the required software and drivers. 5. Power on the robotic arm and follow the initial setup instructions. 6. Begin using the robotic arm for AI education, research, or practical applications as described in the product details.

      2. Manufacturer's minimum age: 18 years 4 months
